State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5662          322 5660            322 5901            322 6441            322 6444


September 25, 1981


Frederick K. Lowell
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
P.O. Box 7880
San Francisco, CA   94120

Dear Fred:

This is in response to your letter dated September 2, 1981 in which you
asked about the reporting of independent expenditures pursuant to the
lobbyist disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Past Commission advice indicates that independent expenditures are
reportable expenditures for persons who file reports pursuant to Government Code Section 86108 (see enclosed advice letter dated June 24,
1977).

In reviewing that past advice, the Commission staff continues to agree
with the conclusion that the intent of Government Code Section 86109 is
to obtain disclosure of all payments made for political purposes by the
filer, and therefore independent expenditures are reportable. However,
the staff is currently proposing a regulation to clarify the necessity
for persons reporting pursuant to Section 86108 to report independent
expenditures.

If I can be of any further assistance to you, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Yours Very Truly,


Lynn Montgomery
Political Reform Consultant

Enc.

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 507 · SACRAMENTO, 95801 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95

June 24, 1977

Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.
Dobbs, Doyle & Nielsen
One Maritime Plaza, Suite 2500
San Francisco, Ca. 94111

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

Thank you for your letter of June 2, 1977, requesting
advice with respect to your reporting obligations. This
letter will confirm the advice which I provided to you over
the telephone on June 15.

The facts as I understand them are as follows: Your
law firm files Form 650 as required by Government Code Sections 86108(c) and 86109.1/ You have asked whether the firm
must report the value of legal or campaign services performed
without cost for the benefit of a candidate or committee on
your Form 650.

Before answering your question with respect to disclosure of contributions pursuant to Section 86109(f), a discussion of some pertinent background information is appropriate.
The Commission has defined the term "contribution" in 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18215 for the purpose of disclosure. Pursuant to Chapter 4 of the Act, the regulation provides that a
contribution includes any payment made to or at the behest of
a controlled committee, the official arm of a political party,
a primary purpose committee or a candidate unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that the payment was received by him in his personal capacity unrelated to his status
as a candidate or officeholder. Subsection (c) of the regulation provides that a payment is made at the behest of a person
whenever a contribution is under his control or made at his
direction. Since every contribution potentially is the subject of disclosure by both the donor and the donee, "contribution" had to be interpreted so as not to require the imposed
donor to disclose the items or services which he did not know he
had received. Thus, contributions are those political benefits

1/
All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise noted.

TWO
24, 1977

over which the donee exercises control whereas independent
expenditures are those political benefits over which he exer-
cises no control. Both types of payments must be reported by
the donor but only contributions need be reported by the donee.

Section 36109(f) requires lobbyists' employers and
other persons attempting to influence legislative or admini-
strative action to disclose all contributions made during the
filing period. Unlike Chapter 4 which applies to both donors
and donees, this provision applies only to persons making con-
tributions. Because this provision imposes no obligations on
the recipients' contributions, the need for a respective
interpretation of "contribution" is not present. Moreover,
we think that the intent of Section 36109(f) was to obtain dis-
closure of all payments made for a political purpose by the
giver. Accordingly, we conclude that "contribution" refers to
any payment made to or on behalf of or for the purpose of
benefiting or opposing any candidate, measure or.committee.
Thus, donations of legal services which would constitute in-
kind contributions or independent expenditures in the chapter
4 sense must be reported by your law firm on its 650 report.

As you know, Section 82015 expressly excludes volun-
teer personal services from the definition of contribution.
In addition, the Commission has construed this exclusion to be
equally applicable with respect to independent expenditures.
Thus volunteer personal services need not be reported on your
Form 650. In addition, you need not report general overhead
expenses nor employee salaries if the employee spends less than
10 percent of his compensated time performing political activi-
ties. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18424 and 18225 and opinion requested by W. Dean Cannon, A FPPC Opinions 133 (76-001,
Aug. 3, 1976). If the attorney or the law firm incurs any ad-
ditional expenses, they must be reported as contributions pur-
suant to Sections 84200, et seq.

If such contributions equal $500 or more in a calen-
dar year, the law firm or attorney will be a committee pursuant
to Section 82013(b) and will be required to file campaign state-
ments pursuant to Section 84200.

If you have any further questions please do not hesi-
tate to call me.

Sincerely,

[illegible]
Ted Prim
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

TP:jo

LAW OFFICES OF

PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO P C

LONDON

225 BUSH STREET

LONDON E.C.2R 1AT
LONDON SWI E 6 OU
TELEPHONE (01) 628 4521

POST OFFICE BOX 7880
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94120

WASHINGTON, D.C.
1660 L STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20036
TELEPHONE (202) 887-0300

CABLE ADDRESS "VANS"
TELEX 278242
TELECOPIER (415) 398-2096

TELEPHONE (415) 983-1000

LOS ANGELES
700 SOUTH FLOWER STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 688-7100

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

SAN JOSE
101 PARK CENTER PLAZA
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95113
TELEPHONE (408) 287-2833

983-1586

September 2, 1981

Ms. Lynn Montgomery
Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Lynn:

This constitutes a request for written advice on
the reportability of independent expenditures under the
lobbying disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act
of 1974 ("The Act").

Government Code section 86108 provides that
lobbyist employers and "$2,500 filers" must file quarterly
reports disclosing the information set forth in section
86109. Section 86109 provides that such reports must con-
tain, inter alia, "the date and amount of each contribution
made by the filer and the name of the recipient of each con-
tribution." (Gov't.Code § 86109(f), (emphasis added).

"Contribution" is a term of art which is defined in
section 82015 and 2 Cal.Admin.Code section 18215. The terms
"expenditure" and "independent expenditure" are also terms of
art which are separately defined (Government Code § 82025,
82031, 2 Cal.Admin.Code § 18225).

An analysis of the foregoing definitions clearly
leads to the conclusion that although all "contributions" are
"expenditures" under the Act, not all "expenditures" are
"contributions." Specifically, "independent expenditures" are
not "contributions" because they are not made to or at the
behest of a candidate or committee (Gov't.Code § 82015, 82031).

[illegible handwritten notes at bottom of page]

Since section 86109 only requires the disclosure
of "contributions", I would appreciate it if you would con-
firm my conclusion that "independent expenditures" are not
reportable by persons filing reports under Government Code
section 86108.

Very truly yours,

Frederick K. Lowell
for
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro


-2-
